articles.title: 1932 - An Athenian Woman Moves Objects from a Distance
articles.excerpt: A report on the telekinetic abilities of the Athenian Kleio Georgiou under the supervision of Tanagras.

content.value: Strange Phenomena
Athens (from our correspondent)

-Most recently, once again the Greek Society of Psychophysiology, whose activities are directed by Mr. Angelos Tanagras, has discovered a remarkable telekinetic medium — that is, a young Athenian woman who possesses the extraordinary gift of moving various objects from a distance, without touching them at all.

What is curious and worthy of study in these phenomena is the verification of whether the medium can produce them at will. Without intending to, these peculiar mediums cause strange movements of objects, breakages, and the like. And all of this typically occurs when everyone's attention is directed elsewhere. Thus these phenomena are usually apparent only from their results. Experiments are now being conducted, however, to determine whether such phenomena can occur at the medium's will and in the presence of others. Such experiments have recently been carried out by the Greek Society of Psychophysiology with the newly discovered medium.

This refined young woman, who proved to be a remarkable medium, is the daughter of a distinguished Athenian family, of excellent upbringing, and presents herself under the pseudonym Kleio Georgiou. This fine young lady suffered a severe psychological shock, having lost simultaneously her beloved father and her sister. Subsequently, as a result of this emotional disturbance, telekinetic phenomena began to occur around her. Objects would frequently leave one room and travel to another, or be found beneath other things — and countless other similar phenomena besides.

This young woman, then, in whose presence these phenomena occurred thanks to her mysterious powers, was invited by Mr. A. Tanagras to attempt, by an act of will and at a specific moment, to move objects without of course touching them. The effort was directed at moving a naval compass and small balls enclosed under a metal bell. Miss Kleio persisted in the attempt for many days, and indeed, as confirmed by Mr. Tanagras, she ultimately succeeded in moving the needle of the naval compass.

A small compass was immediately placed before her and set upon a table. Miss Kleio then held her open hands above the compass and moved them right and left, at a distance of up to a hand's span, while concentrating her thoughts. And so, after a few moments of focused concentration, she managed to move the compass needle without touching it — by as much as 90 degrees, that is, a quarter of the full circle. This was repeated several times. And not only that: she managed on occasion to turn the needle through the full circle of the compass.

Mr. Tanagras then asked the young woman to place her hands beneath the table. And the result was the same. When Kleio moved her hands to place her fingers below the table, the needle once again made complete revolutions. The compass was then placed beneath two glass covers. Even so, the result remained the same. On some occasions it was exactly alike, on others somewhat weaker.

These truly astonishing phenomena, having been verified and confirmed, were repeated before a larger circle of individuals at Society gatherings for the telepathy experiments between Athens and London — which we have already described.

Mr. Tanagras then explained the great significance of the phenomenon and invited those present to try to do what Miss Kleio had done. It goes without saying that no one achieved any result. The gracious young woman, for the sake of scientific verification of the phenomena, asked the hostess of the house where the experiments were being conducted to search and examine her...
Part 1

articles.title: 1932 - Medium Zoe Voudouri — Certificate & Newspaper Allagi
articles.excerpt: An announcement regarding the medium Zoe Voudouri, who demonstrates phenomena of hypnotic hyper-intensification and telepathy. Readers of the newspaper «Allagi» may submit questions by presenting 5 issues, with answers to be published in a dedicated column.

content.value: The scientific world and the educated public stand astonished before the unprecedented phenomenon of the new Medium Zoe Voudouri. The medium in question, under a special contract, has undertaken the obligation to participate every Monday in dedicated spiritualist séances held for the benefit of the readers of «ALLAGI» and within that newspaper's offices.
Each male or female reader of «Allagi» who presents 5 issues of the newspaper will be entitled to ask two questions concerning their work and affairs, etc.
What Mr. Tanagras Says About the New Medium
SOCIETY OF PSYCHOPHYSIOLOGY — CERTIFICATE
It is hereby certified that ZOE VOUDOURI demonstrates the following extraordinary phenomena:
a.) Hypnosis of post-spiritual hyper-intensification
b.) Hypnosis and post-hypnotic suggestion
c.) Telepathy
The present certificate is issued for her use before the Authorities and the local Medical and Scientific Associations.
The President, A. Tanagras
For Provincial Readers
Readers will cut the datelines from ten consecutive issues of ALLAGI and deliver them to the Press Agency of their city, receiving a corresponding receipt.
This receipt is to be enclosed in an envelope together with the datelines of the issues delivered and their two questions, signing their letter with a pseudonym or their initials.
The answer will be given in the relevant column of the newspaper.

articles.title: 1959 - Advertisement for Medium Elpida Pavlatou
articles.excerpt: Elpida Pavlatou was awarded a prize for long-distance telepathy by the Society for Psychical Research. Experiments with a scientific basis were conducted in collaboration with international societies. She receives visitors daily at 6 Kritis Street, Athens.

content.value: Diploma and Prize for Long-Distance Telepathy from the Society for Psychical Research, in recognition of her outstanding performance in experiments conducted by Mr. Tanagras and the foreign Societies of Vienna, Tübingen, London, New York, and with Professors Gardner Murphy and Esterreich of various universities. She has also demonstrated phenomena of psychometry (Epipsychidion).
Performs all experiments that have a scientific basis. Receives visitors daily 9 a.m.–1 p.m. and 4–7 p.m. 6 Kritis Street, side street off Liosion, Plateia Vathis.

articles.excerpt: Spiritual services for matters of the heart, predictions and disappearances. Scientific horoscope prepared on the basis of date of birth. Letters with a stamped reply envelope to be sent to Mrs. Toulan T., 14 Patission Street, 7th floor, Athens.

content.value: Effective psycho-spiritual experiments at any distance. Answers whatever concerns you. Matters of the heart of all kinds, marriage, nervous conditions, predictions, disappearances, the deceased, lost objects, etc. Suggestion, autosuggestion, remote influence by psychic projection under hypnosis. Additionally, learn the course of your life through a scientific horoscope, prepared on the basis of your date and year of birth. For letters from within the country and abroad, for information send us a stamped reply envelope. Address: Mrs. Toulan T., 14 Patission Street, 7th floor.
Athens

articles.title: A New Medium — Miss Daphne, 1940
articles.author: Άγγελος Τανάγρας
articles.excerpt: The Society for Psychical Research presents the new medium Daphne.

image.alt: Daphne — new medium of the Society for Psychical Research

content.value: For years now, one of our finest Pythias, Mrs. Pavlatou, had been bringing her little niece with her to the Society. About ten years old, a fair-haired little doll, whom everyone admired and whose hair they would ruffle affectionately.
…And then suddenly, like a storm cloud, the news fell upon our circles that the beautiful little Greek doll was gravely ill with meningitis.

What she suffered and how she was saved need not concern us here. What matters is that she was saved. But with her recovery came another interesting phenomenon. It happens sometimes that in individuals with a hereditary predisposition, chiefly after diseases of the brain, intuitive faculties begin to manifest themselves — telepathy, clairvoyance, and the like.

In this case, the hereditary disposition was present, since her aunt on the maternal side is one of the finest Greek Pythias. The irritation of the meninges did the rest. And the little doll began to give strong indications of her talent.
And what a coincidence! Daphne was also the name of one of the finest ancient Pythias of the Oracle at Delphi.

Naturally, once her health was restored, she was enrolled in the circle of intuitives of the Society for Psychical Research and her apprenticeship began. Today, her gift has developed sufficiently that she can safely work before a large audience. In the past year she already participated in long-distance telepathy experiments with England and Germany, placing among the top performers; and Professor Esterreich of the University of Tübingen and Mr. Herbert, director of the experiments of the English Society for Psychical Research, both remarked particularly on her success.
The Society for Psychical Research presents her with pleasure, grateful that the blood and sacred tradition of the ancient Pythias has not yet run dry in our race.

articles.title: A Trial Following a Medium's Revelations — 1934
articles.excerpt: Before the Court of Appeal, the discovery of the perpetrators of a suitcase theft aboard a steamship was certified — a revelation made by the medium Mrs. Zakynthinou.

image.alt: A trial concerning a medium's revelations

content.value: A Trial with the Medium's Revelations as Its Subject

In the meantime, here in Greece, thanks to the Society for Psychical Research and its president Mr. Tanagras, none of the above (an article about a hypnosis machine in Paris) seems at all strange, since we have reached the point where criminals are identified by mediums.

One such feat, accomplished by Mrs. Zakynthinou — who, as our readers will recall, some three months ago identified the perpetrators of a suitcase theft aboard the steamship "Kefallineia" — was certified yesterday before the Court of Appeal, where the appeal of one of the thieves, by the name of "Sigalas", was being heard. He had been convicted following his arrest and sentenced to five months' imprisonment. Yesterday Sigalas confessed to the theft, while Mr. A. Venetis, the victim of the theft, recounted how the medium — after being hypnotised — had guided him, saying:

— The suitcase was broken into aboard the steamship Kefallineia at the time of its transfer. There are 2 perpetrators. One is about 25 years old, red-haired and fair. The second is about 38 years old, short and slight, and goes by the name Giannis. These two appear to be on the same shift. It is between 2 and 3 in the morning. The items were placed inside the funnel. At 5 in the morning both left. They took tram No. 20. At the end of the stop they headed in the direction of Kritika. In a hovel indicated to them the previous day by a young woman (the young man's fiancée) and an old woman, the division of the goods took place. Short Giannis took half the goods and made his way towards the Taboureia neighbourhood.

Based on the above, the two thieves were arrested, but one escaped. Before the court, Mr. Tanagras explained the phenomenon scientifically, expounding that Zakynthinou had done nothing other than receive the thoughts of the thieves, who were surely thinking intensely about their deed.

articles.title: Discovery of a Theft by a Medium — Kymi, 1955
articles.author: Άγγελος Τανάγρας
articles.excerpt: An unprecedented case of theft discovery in Kymi, Euboea, through telepathically synchronised communication with the medium «SOFIA – KASSANDRA», who revealed the perpetrator, the location of the stolen goods, and his kinship with the victim.

content.value: The Society for Psychical Research received from the Officer of the Royal Gendarmerie of Kymi, Euboea, the following document concerning the discovery of a theft by a medium.
HELLENIC ROYAL GENDARMERIE — KYMI GENDARMERIE POST
In Kymi, 10 August 1955
Esteemed Mr. Tanagras,

By means of the present letter we hasten to bring to your attention the astonishing success of the medium «SOFIA – KASSANDRA», a member of your Society who was at the time staying in Kymi, and whose invaluable contribution led to the discovery of the perpetrator of a theft committed in the village of Oxylithos in our district, which lies approximately 15 kilometres from the Police Station.
This discovery was achieved through the medium's generous, spontaneous and disinterested cooperation with the officers of the Gendarmerie in locating the perpetrator.
Our service, following the details provided by the medium:
The description and location of the house where the theft took place,
The spot where the perpetrator had concealed the stolen goods (inside a field near a mulberry tree, beside a stream and beneath a pile of stones),
The perpetrator's name «ANTONIOS» and the detail that he is related to the victim,
contributed to the discovery and arrest of the perpetrator within the shortest time, who fully confessed his guilt.

The astonishing success of the medium, the detailed description of the events of the committed theft in the manner of a re-enactment, and above all the positive naming of the perpetrator as «ANTONIOS», made a deep impression upon us. We hope that future cooperation between your Society and the security forces would yield invaluable results for society at large.
The Commander of the Post (T.S.) M. Grigorakos
The explanation already relates to the reception of thought (telepathy) from the thief by Kassandra. As is known, in order to achieve this the following are required:
A kind of organisational affinity between the medium and the thief,
A kind of wavelength affinity of the psychic wave, analogous to the electromagnetic waves of the radio (tuning), without which the transmission of thought is impossible.

In this case, Kassandra was tuned to the thief and received his thoughts, like an open radio receiver. It should be noted that the stronger the medium, the more easily it tunes in and receives thoughts.
In similar cases where no tuning existed — as in antiquity between the Pythia and Apollo — the priests would intervene and deliver ambiguous oracles, so that the God would not appear to be lying.
(Signed: ANGELOS TANAGRAS)

articles.title: Eleni Kikidou — A New Medium of the SPR, 1933
articles.author: Νικ. Παπ
articles.excerpt: The Society for Psychical Research presents Miss Eleni Kikidou, a new medium who has achieved success in telepathy and clairvoyance experiments.

image.alt: Eleni Kikidou — New medium of the SPR

content.value: Beware of Charlatans Who Present Themselves as Mediums — Always Seek Information from the Society for Psychical Research — Genuine mediums are trained first by the SPR — A new medium: Miss Kikidou

It was inevitable that the business of mediums would not escape exploitation by various charlatans, who realised there was money to be made in this line of work. There is, moreover, the reassurance that those who resort to mediums typically do not report whatever may befall them, out of fear of causing a scandal. On the other hand, such charlatans contribute irreparably to the loss of public confidence in genuine scientific mediums.
For these scientific mediums — those officially recognised — have formed the Association of Scientific Intuitive Mediums, which has issued the following characteristic announcement:

“The Association of Scientific Intuitives (mediums) draws the public's attention to the various charlatans who advertise themselves as mediums in the newspapers with fantastical promotions claiming to reveal secrets, cure diseases, etc., with the aim of deceiving the credulous.

The Association will take measures in every case against these charlatans, notifying newspapers and police authorities in writing, and provisionally recommends that, for any medium, information should be sought from the Society for Psychical Research (at Tanagras's residence).

The sphere of activity of the scientific mediums of the Society for Psychical Research extends only to telepathy, clairvoyance and psychometry, of which only the first admits positive and direct verification. The results of the other two would be best regarded merely as indications in need of further verification.”

And yet there are so many officially recognised mediums that the charlatans would have no place at all — if, of course, there were no gullible people, those impulsive souls who, the moment they think they want to learn something from a medium, rush off without first enquiring wherever they happen to find one.

Every so often the Society for Psychical Research, whose president is Mr. Tanagras, issues publications concerning the activities of its mediums, members of the SPR, and the progress of psychical research — which makes it entirely inexcusable for anyone to fall victim to charlatans masquerading as mediums. And every so often, after a series of trials that may last as long as a year, new mediums are recognised, so that there should be no need for such charlatans to exist.

Today, at the offices of the SPR in Athens, there are approximately 20 capable mediums. Among them, as of a few days ago, is Miss Eleni Kikidou — a fair-haired girl with beautiful, expressive blue eyes. For six months without interruption she took part in the experiments of the SPR, and she has now been granted a licence for public appearances.

Miss Kikidou achieved successes in the telepathy experiments as well as in the clairvoyance experiments. It is worth saying a few words about these latter experiments, in which not all mediums succeed. In these, the hypnotised medium attempts to identify the contents of unknown sealed envelopes. The experiments are conducted in the following manner, as described to us by Mr. Tanagras:
There is always a set number of envelopes at the offices of the SPR, each containing a word, a name, a number, etc., wrapped in opaque paper.

One of these envelopes is drawn at random and placed in the hands of the hypnotised medium, who places it on the forehead and chest and concentrates. Within a period of less than three minutes, the medium must identify the exact contents. How they achieve this in such an experiment is still unknown. It is presumably a matter of living X-rays, but conscious X-rays — for having penetrated the envelope and found the contents, they return to inform the medium's brain.

Eleni Kikidou, whose experiments we have personally attended, achieves an 80% success rate — much as, six years ago, another medium named Maria Athanasopoulos did, who was the first in Greece to produce psychometric phenomena.

Moreover, the 14-year-old Kikidou also demonstrates vivid post-hypnotic suggestion, registering remarkable successes in the Athens–Germany telepathic experiments in which Professor Esterreich participated — as we had already written in the Akropolis.
In general, Mr. Tanagras believes she is an exceptional medium.

articles.title: Eleni Kikidou — Presentation of SPR Experiments, 1934
articles.excerpt: The Society for Psychical Research presented new experiments in telepathy, post-hypnotic suggestion and psychometry, with the participation of Eleni Kikidou and other mediums.

image.alt: Eleni Kikidou in experiments of the SPR

content.value: The New Experiments of the Society for Psychical Research — What Mr. Tanagras Says

At the Society for Psychical Research, in a session held the previous Tuesday, Angelos Tanagras proceeded to conduct experiments with the Society's mediums: Miss Kassandra, Eleni Zakynthinou, Elpida Pavlatou and Eleni Kikidou.
Initially, telepathy experiments were carried out successfully.
Mr. Tanagras hypnotised three of his mediums — Kassandra, Pavlatou and Zakynthinou — who successfully executed the thought-transmission experiments.

The visitors who sat beside the mediums without, however, coming into direct contact with them, concentrated their thoughts upon certain images from their lives — incidents, adventures of their youth or things that had made a great impression upon them. The mediums managed, while under hypnosis, to follow the thoughts of the visitors and tell them precisely what they were thinking.
A post-hypnotic suggestion experiment with Miss Eleni Kikidou followed. Mr. Tanagras hypnotised her and instructed her to sit in the chair next to a «Metropolitan Bishop».

The medium moved respectfully towards the empty seat beside her, bowed reverently, and begged as a favour from the imaginary Metropolitan Bishop to be allowed to kiss his hand. At that moment, one of the SPR members, Mr. Patriarcheas, picked up his own chair and placed it next to hers. The medium was visibly scandalised by the disrespect shown by Mr. Patriarcheas towards the imaginary Metropolitan and began crossing herself. Mr. Tanagras then informed her that the journalist writing this article was… her mother.

The medium turned and looked at me while I was smoking — something that clearly shocked her — and began to protest to her «mother» because she «was smoking in front of father». The consequences of all this were borne by my cigarette packet, as the girl saw fit to confiscate it so that her mother would not smoke!

Finally, the third part of the experiments was carried out. Misses Kassandra, Zakynthinou and Pavlatou, under hypnosis, performed psychometry experiments. They were given by one of the visitors three identical pieces of cardboard, which the mediums pressed against all their surfaces. The cardboard pieces were then marked with a barely visible, very discreet sign so they could be identified, and subsequently mixed in with other pieces of cardboard and returned to the mediums. After a few minutes of tactile examination, the mediums found the pieces of cardboard they had originally handled, keeping their eyes closed throughout the entire experiment.
Mr. Tanagras explains the experiment to us a little further and says:

The skin, it seems, leaves upon various objects it touches a particular substance — invisible not only to the naked eye but also under the microscope. This substance is probably immaterial, since we cannot observe its form. When these objects are returned to the mediums, they are able to recognise the immaterial trace. The question of touch is not a serious one to discuss, since all the pieces of cardboard are identical and of the same colour.

articles.title: Revelations of the Medium Zoe — 1933
articles.excerpt: The 14-year-old Zoe Voudouri, a new medium of extraordinary power, causes a sensation in spiritist circles with successful predictions, telepathic experiments and political prophecies.

content.value: Around the subject of spiritualism a number of misunderstandings have arisen. Some maintain that any search for a scientific explanation of spiritualist phenomena is superfluous, while others deny even the reality of the experiments themselves, attributing their apparent accuracy to tricks or deceptions on the part of the mediums, etc.

We do not believe that spiritualism reduces to the existence of supernatural phenomena. All the various spiritualist phenomena can be easily and fully explained, provided we admit the intervention of conscious spirits which bring them about.
Spiritualism was known from the most ancient times. The history of modern spiritualism begins in 1841, and the first psychical research was conducted in America.

At the outset their explanation was attributed to supernatural forces; however, the psychophysiologists later disagreed with this view — for while they acknowledge as indisputable the phenomena observed by the spiritualists, they disagree as to their interpretation.
They deny, that is, the existence and intervention of spirits, arguing that the phenomena are due to the emission of an animal force which they call psychodynamism.

In Greece, the investigation of psychical and generally spiritualist phenomena began at the turn of the century. In 1900 the «Society for Psychical Research» was founded by Mr. Tanagras and a group of scientists.
Today the Society, which represents the science of spiritualism in Greece, accepts almost entirely hypnotism and telepathic phenomena.
And it has made considerable progress by making use of individuals who possess to an extraordinary degree the faculty of suggestion and hypnosis — the so-called mediums.

Most recently, on Greece's spiritual horizon, there has appeared a new and extraordinarily powerful medium: the 14-year-old Zoe Voudouri, whose astonishing experiments have caused a sensation among the circles of psychical researchers.

She has been examined by many who occupy themselves with psychical phenomena and has been used in public psychic gatherings. Mr. Tanagras has granted her a certificate in which he recognises the following faculties:
1) hypnosis with mnemonic hyper-intensification, 2) hypnotic and post-hypnotic suggestion, and 3) telepathy.
This exceptional medium is touring Greece, and has been in our city for three days.
We met her at the «Tourist» hotel, where she is staying with her father, Ch. Voudouri, and the hypnotist Mr. Morsy.
At first glance, nothing about this little girl suggests she possesses such extraordinary spiritual «gifts».
Unlike most mediums, she is very fair-haired. Yet the pallor of her face and her large eyes with their distant expression give the medium away.
She tells us about her life. How her gift was discovered. She even foretells future events for us.
Her gift was revealed under tragic circumstances. On Good Friday of 1930 she announced the death of her uncle, which was confirmed the following day.
Her father brought her to Athens and she was examined by Mr. Tanagras at the University in May 1931, before professors, who confirmed that she was an exceptional medium.
Her fame spread through collaboration with an Athenian newspaper and through successful experiments, increasing the paper's circulation by 3,000 copies.
Her greatest success was finding a living mother of a young man who believed her to be dead — a fact that was fully confirmed.
Zoe has predicted electoral results with absolute accuracy, as well as the Plastiras revolution in detail.
She also predicted the return of a lost dog in seven minutes — and this too was confirmed.
Regarding political developments, she predicted the fall of the government within a year and the death of a person of the highest rank.
We take our leave of this remarkable figure, hoping that the second political prediction will not come to pass.
Source: Neologos Patron, 12.05.1933

articles.title: A Medium Had Foreseen the Deaths of the Greek Prime Ministers — 1936
articles.excerpt: The medium Eleftheria Skariotou had predicted, on New Year's Eve of 1936, the successive deaths of four Prime Ministers, as well as bloody incidents in Athens before the elections.

image.alt: Archival image of the article from the newspaper Tachydromos, 1936

content.value: With the death the day before yesterday of the President of the Government, Greece loses within the first four months of 1936 three of its Prime Ministers. In order of their deaths: G. Kondylis, E. Venizelos and K. Demertzis.

Remarkable, however, is the fact that the successive deaths of these Prime Ministers were predicted by an Athens medium. Eleftheria Skariotou had been visited on New Year's Eve by a journalist who asked her at the time for predictions for the new year.
Up to this moment the medium's predictions have been verified one hundred percent and are causing astonishment.

A series of questions had been put to Eleftheria Skariotou, and among the first was whether the elections that had been announced for the previous January — and which were said at the time to be facing postponement — would in fact take place.
— The elections will be held on the scheduled date, the medium replied.
And she added: — But one week before the elections, bloody scenes will occur in Athens, in which at least one person will be killed and many will be wounded.
And indeed, one week before the elections, the scenes unfolded on Emm. Benaki Street, in which the student Voutsinas was killed and others were wounded.

But the most astonishing thing is that Eleftheria Skariotou predicted the successive deaths of the Prime Ministers — the only difference being that, until now, the fulfilment of her predictions has not been completed in its entirety.
— During 1936, the medium had said, many deaths of our country's political figures will be recorded.
— Can you not, she was asked, specify your predictions more clearly?
Skariotou appeared to be thinking, and then replied: — Four Prime Ministers will die.
Further details, such as names, etc., she was unable or unwilling to provide.

So far, with the successive deaths of the Prime Ministers, the medium's prediction has been verified to three-quarters of its extent. The question now arising is: will the macabre prediction of the medium be verified in full? Are we to mourn the death of a fourth Prime Minister?
To this question only time can give a clear answer. And more than 8 months of 1936 still remain.
Note: That same year P. Tsaldaris also died, thus completing 4 prime ministerial deaths in 1936.
Source: Tachydromos, 16.04.1936

seo.metaTitle: A Medium Had Foreseen the Deaths of the Greek Prime Ministers (1936) | Mediums of the Society | Society for Psychical Research | haunted.gr

articles.title: Medium Karyati Senses Earthquakes — 1933
articles.excerpt: Ioanna Karyati, a recognised medium of the Society for Psychical Research, had a premonition of the devastating earthquakes in Kos and Chalkidiki hours before they struck, and simultaneously located the death of an elderly man in Thessaloniki.

image.alt: Archival image of the article from the newspaper Ethnikos Kiryx, 1933

content.value: Telepathic mediums are numerous — perhaps as numerous as the people of delicate psychic disposition and sensibility. They remain unknown, however, since they do not succeed in recognising their own telepathic abilities, which typically, if not almost always, appear suddenly and entirely by chance. Among the known telepathic mediums, in any case, very few have been recognised by the Society for Psychical Research, presided over by Mr. Angelos Tanagras.
And among those few, again, the ones with absolute successes in experiments can be counted on the fingers of one hand.

Among this last group is Mrs. Ioanna Karyati (this is her pseudonym, and she took it in all probability from Kariatidos Street No. 10, where she lives), of whom Mr. Tanagras had spoken with great enthusiasm a month ago. He said at the time that Mrs. Ioanna Karyati possessed exceptional telepathic faculties — the kind, in fact, that are found only in first-rate mediums. Today, however, this medium, who moreover reads your thoughts even while you are awake, also displays curious magnetic properties worthy of special study.
Whole hours before the earthquakes that devastated Kos, she had a premonition of them.

On the morning of the day of the earthquakes, to be precise, she started up in terror and told those present — a Mr. and Mrs. Vergoulas — that she had a premonition of a devastating earthquake. One might think it mere coincidence, were it not for the fact that a few days earlier she had also had a premonition of a new earthquake in Chalkidiki — again, many hours before it happened. She was travelling by train from Thessaloniki to Athens. Before reaching Larissa, she announced to her fellow passengers, aghast, that there would be an earthquake — and they were dumbfounded later, after many hours had passed, when they learned that in Chalkidiki, 3 to 4 hours after her premonition, a powerful earthquake had struck.

On another occasion, during her stay in Thessaloniki, while she was chatting with friends in the hotel where she was lodging, she suddenly took on an expression of anguish. For a moment she seemed like a different person standing before the company — one who had fixed her gaze elsewhere entirely. Then everyone heard her say clearly:
— Run! Help! The... elderly man is dying!
Where? Who? Nobody knew. But Mrs. Karyati rose and told her friends:
— In the building at the corner of Kolombou and Egnatia Streets, an old man has just died.
And indeed, within a few minutes this was confirmed. At the entrance of the building they found the daughter-in-law of the deceased, Melfou, still crying out and begging for help.

On this occasion, let us also mention a remarkable observation made by another, equally well-known medium — one not belonging to the Society for Psychical Research — according to whom the spirit of the late «teacher» Vdelopoulos has passed into the leader of the blue-and-whites, Armando Delapatridis, through whom it has formed an unbreakable friendship at «Daphni» — where, Delapatridis now claims, it was agreed that as soon as the leader of the blue-and-whites takes power, the archbishopric throne will be granted to Vdelopoulos.

And this medium's observation rests on the fact that Delapatridis was seized, precisely at the moment of the «teacher's» death, by a religious frenzy identical to that of Vdelopoulos — even down to the very phrases used.

Those who have recently encountered Delapatridis at Zacharatos's café or at the Zappeion will have heard him singing psalms or preaching the word of God — precisely as the «teacher» used to preach, and with the same exact refrain:
— Woe unto the accursed! Into the outer fire, ye devils!

One would think one was listening to Vdelopoulos. And even seeing him, when Delapatridis moves about nervously and waves his walking stick threateningly in the air — all to ward off an enthusiastic cuff from the irreverent rabble.
N.K.
Source: Ethnikos Kiryx, 06.06.1933
